commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair lane is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the intentional CLI compatibility break and ordinary merge gating.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern was found; the diff touches Go command logic, tests, command docs, and changelog text without new dependencies, secrets handling, or automation changes. commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `docs cell-style` from zero-based to one-based table, row, and column coordinates, adds negative table-index support with resolved output, and updates tests plus command docs/changelog text.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main documents and implements `docs cell-style` as zero-based while adjacent Docs table commands use one-based coordinates with negative table indexes.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the intentional CLI compatibility break and ordinary merge gating.

target.title: fix(docs): align cell-style coordinates
target.body:
## Summary

- make `docs cell-style` use one-based table, row, and column coordinates like adjacent Docs table commands
- accept negative table indexes counting from the end
- report the resolved positive table index after a real mutation
- regenerate command docs and document the coordinate migration

## Compatibility

This intentionally corrects the prior inconsistent zero-based interface. Existing `docs cell-style` invocations must add one to positive table, row, and column values. Table index `0` is now invalid.

Maintainer decision: accept this documented breaking migration rather than retain a compatibility shim for the isolated zero-based behavior.

## Verification

- `go test ./internal/cmd -run 'TestDocs(CellStyle|TableColumnWidth)' -count=1`
- `make docs-check`
- rebased exact head `8a67ffd1`
- `make ci` after final rebase
- structured autoreview after final rebase: clean, no actionable findings (confidence 0.86)
- live `clawdbot@gmail.com` proof:
  - created a disposable Doc with two 2x2 tables
  - styled table `1`, row `1`, column `1` red
  - styled table `-1`, row `2`, column `2` blue and bold
  - command output resolved `-1` to table index `2`
  - raw Docs API readback confirmed both exact cells and styles
  - permanently deleted the fixture
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
